closed_reason: A4 closeout-hygiene planning converged; freeze execution deferred to a dedicated follow-up session from c6b0adc
task_summary: A4 close-ack OBJECT follow-up: diagnose git-frozen, artifact-provenance, and worktree-hygiene issues without re-opening A4. Turn 5 absorbed Turn 4 amendments (live counts 16M+8??, Document/dialogue/state.json inclusion, G5 snapshot correction, canonical SHA c6b0adc, commit 3 deferral, main-vs-c6b0adc rationale) and closed the hygiene-planning session as execute-ready. Freeze execution deferred to a dedicated follow-up session from c6b0adc.

contract_checkpoints.C1: Lock the live baseline for the hygiene problem. A4 remains converged and frozen; the hygiene session records actual git/worktree state (16 modified + 8 untracked status entries at Turn 5).
contract_checkpoints.C2: Freeze provenance uses canonical SHA c6b0adc (artifact side) + planned anchor-freeze commit on a new codex/a4-closeout-freeze branch. sha256 manifest is deferred. 0c078ea is a README-only sibling.
contract_checkpoints.C3: Measurement-path scope remains the 5-file anchor set. Hygiene session directory + Document/dialogue/state.json travel together as commit 2. Everything else stays G-UNRELATED-DIRTY.
contract_checkpoints.C4: G3 = freeze-commit SHA OR sha256 manifest (OR, not AND). G5 = before/after status-entry diff; commits 1+2 must clean exactly 7 entries (5 anchors + root state + hygiene dir), none added.
contract_checkpoints.C5: Execute order B -> C -> A with freeze execution deferred. Turn 5 closes this session as execute-ready planning; the follow-up session will perform the actual freeze commits.

decisions: Opened session 2026-04-15-a4-closeout-hygiene as a strict plan-only follow-up to the A4 close-ack OBJECT verdict.
Turn 1 proposed C1-C5 plus G1-G5 and execute options A/B/C without editing code, git state, or frozen A4 artifacts.

Turn 2 peer review amended the baseline to 15 modified and 12 untracked paths, clarified G3 as freeze-commit OR sha256-manifest, clarified G5 as an allowlist diff, and preferred execute ordering B -> C -> A.

Turn 3 (claude-code) executed option B in full and option C as a plan-only draft. Recorded drift from Turn 2: HEAD advanced b62ada1 -> c6b0adc, branch renamed codex/phase-a1-token-logging -> codex/local-dirty-preserved, and c6b0adc already landed DAD scaffolding + A4 result artifacts but NOT the 5 measurement anchors. Freeze plan drafted as 3 commits on a new codex/a4-closeout-freeze branch cut from c6b0adc. No git mutation performed.

Turn 4 (codex) peer verification kept the overall freeze direction but found inventory drift in Turn 3 artifacts: live status is 16 modified + 8 untracked entries, Document/dialogue/state.json is omitted from the boundary map and G5 snapshots, and the session should use Turn 5 for one corrective plan-only pass before any freeze-execute session.

Turn 5 (claude-code) absorbed all four Turn 4 amendments (A1 live counts, A2 state.json inclusion, A3 G5 snapshot correction, A4 canonical SHA + commit 3 deferral), rewrote provenance-boundary.md §0/§2.1/§2.2/§3/§5 and freeze-plan.md §0/§1/§2/§5/§7, and closes this session as execute-ready. Freeze execution moves to a new dedicated session (suggested id: 2026-04-15-a4-freeze-execute) cutting codex/a4-closeout-freeze from c6b0adc. No git mutation performed.
meta_improvements: The hygiene problem is larger than the original close-ack wording: Document, tools, prompts, agent contracts, and experiments are all untracked, so the issue is meta-hygiene across A1-A4 plus DAD infrastructure.
The current branch name and HEAD history do not match the effective scope of the work. A dedicated hygiene branch should be considered during execute instead of reusing codex/phase-a1-token-logging.
pipeline/community_matcher.py and pipeline/subtitle_analyzer.py are outside the initial A4 raw-measurement provenance path unless the execute scope is widened to full report-render provenance.
Close-ack ACK should be treated as a separate rubric after the hygiene slice, not assumed from arithmetic correctness alone.

Provenance is asymmetric as of c6b0adc: A4 result artifacts are git-frozen but measurement-time anchor source is not. Freeze rubric must judge result-side and anchor-side separately or it will silently undercount the gap.
Two near-identical commits (c6b0adc and 0c078ea, differing only by README.md) exist; Turn 5 fixed canonical = c6b0adc and demoted 0c078ea to README-only sibling.
Status-entry count (git status --short line count) and expanded-file count (files inside an untracked directory) must be tracked separately; conflating them is what produced Turn 3's 15/12 mistake.

Root Document/dialogue/state.json co-owns the hygiene-session commit rather than a standalone scaffolding commit, because its diff against the artifact-freeze SHA is exactly the hygiene-session state update.
Auth status oscillates per turn; future sessions must re-probe at start rather than assume continuity, and ownership rules should flip dynamically.
